description: Fix new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted') with no category in handleFetchError() — abort errors are not categorized as timeout, so AI agents cannot distinguish them from internal errors.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/gitlab && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace new ToolError('aborted') with ToolError.timeout() in gitlab-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/gitlab/src/gitlab-api.ts, handleFetchError() at line 66 throws `new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted')` with no category set. Abort errors (from browser navigation or explicit signal.abort()) should be categorized as timeouts so AI agents know not to retry. Replace with ToolError.timeout().
acceptanceCriteria: The `new ToolError('Request was aborted', 'aborted')` line in handleFetchError() is replaced with ToolError.timeout('Request was aborted')
No direct ToolError constructor calls remain for abort errors in gitlab-api.ts
